skill.name: prisma-expert
skill.description: Prisma ORM expert for schema design, migrations, query optimization, relations modeling, and database operations across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite.
skill.summary: Expert assistance for Prisma ORM schema design, database migrations, query optimization, and connection management.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 88 patterns that are all false positives for this legitimate Prisma ORM helper skill. External commands are standard Prisma CLI operations (validate, migrate, generate). Network references are documentation URLs. Environment access is limited to DATABASE_URL and NODE_ENV configuration required for database connectivity. No malicious patterns, credential exfiltration, or dangerous code execution detected.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: All commands are legitimate Prisma CLI operations (prisma validate, prisma migrate dev, prisma generate) and standard environment detection (ls, grep) for a database ORM tool.
Environment variable access is limited to NODE_ENV for environment detection and DATABASE_URL for database connection configuration - standard and necessary for a Prisma ORM skill.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: Skill executes shell commands via npx prisma CLI for environment detection and database operations. All commands are legitimate Prisma tooling with hardcoded arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands are hardcoded Prisma CLI operations with no user input injection vectors - safe database tooling pattern.

content.user_title: Master Prisma ORM for Database Development
content.value_statement: Struggling with Prisma schema errors, migration conflicts, or slow database queries? This skill provides expert guidance for schema design, migration management, and query optimization across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite.
actual_capabilities: Design and validate Prisma schemas with proper relations, indexes, and field types
Create, debug, and resolve database migration conflicts
Optimize queries to eliminate N+1 problems and reduce over-fetching
Configure connection pooling for serverless and production environments
Implement transaction patterns with proper isolation levels
Review code for Prisma best practices and anti-patterns
limitations: Cannot directly access or modify your database - provides guidance only
Does not replace database administrators for infrastructure-level configuration
Raw SQL optimization may require postgres-expert or mongodb-expert for complex cases
Cannot execute migrations automatically - user must run Prisma CLI commands

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Developer Building a SaaS Application
use_cases.description: Design a scalable Prisma schema with proper user, subscription, and billing models. Create safe migration workflows for production deployments and optimize queries for dashboard performance.

use_cases.title: Team Lead Resolving Migration Conflicts
use_cases.description: Debug conflicting migrations from multiple developers. Learn to use prisma migrate resolve, squash migration history, and establish team workflows to prevent future conflicts.

use_cases.title: Backend Engineer Optimizing API Performance
use_cases.description: Identify and fix N+1 query problems in REST or GraphQL APIs. Implement proper select and include patterns, add strategic indexes, and configure connection pooling for high-traffic endpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Schema Design Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Prisma schema for best practices. Check relation definitions, index usage, and field types. Suggest improvements for performance and maintainability.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a schema.prisma file and want expert feedback before deploying to production.

prompt_templates.title: Migration Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: My Prisma migration failed with error [paste error]. The database is in an inconsistent state. Help me diagnose the issue and provide step-by-step commands to resolve it.
prompt_templates.scenario: A migration failed during deployment and you need to recover safely.

prompt_templates.title: Query Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: I have this Prisma query that is slow [paste query]. Analyze it for N+1 problems, over-fetching, and missing indexes. Show me the optimized version with explain.
prompt_templates.scenario: Your API endpoint is slow and you suspect database query issues.

prompt_templates.title: Connection Pool Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: I am deploying to [Vercel/AWS Lambda/Render] and getting connection errors. Help me configure Prisma connection pooling for my serverless environment with the correct DATABASE_URL parameters.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production deployment is failing due to connection pool exhaustion.

output_examples.input: Schema with implicit many-to-many relation causing performance issues
output_examples.output: Identified implicit many-to-many relation between User and Post models. Recommendation: Create explicit Join model with additional fields for metadata. Added @relation directives with clear naming convention. Added composite index on foreign keys for join performance.

output_examples.input: N+1 query in user listing endpoint fetching posts individually
output_examples.output: Detected N+1 query pattern. Solution: Replace individual post fetches with single query using include relation. Reduced 101 queries to 1 query. Added select to limit returned fields. Estimated 95% reduction in database round-trips.

best_practices: Always use explicit @relation with fields and references for clarity and to prevent relation resolution issues
Enable query logging in development to identify slow queries and N+1 problems early
Use migrate dev only in development - always use migrate deploy for production with CI/CD pipelines
anti_patterns: Using implicit many-to-many relations when you need to store metadata on the join table
Calling prisma.user.findMany() then looping to fetch related data - causes N+1 queries
Running migrate dev in production which can cause data loss and is not idempotent

faq.question: How do I reset my development database without losing production data?
faq.answer: Use npx prisma migrate reset in development only. This command drops the database, reapplies all migrations, and reseeds. Never run this in production. For production, create a new migration with the required changes instead.

faq.question: What is the difference between prisma migrate dev and prisma migrate deploy?
faq.answer: migrate dev is for development - it creates migration files, applies them, and resets on schema drift. migrate deploy is for production - it only applies pending migrations without creating new files or resetting. Never use migrate dev in production.

faq.question: How do I fix migration conflicts when multiple developers create migrations?
faq.answer: Use prisma migrate resolve to mark conflicting migrations as applied or rolled back. For complex conflicts, you may need to manually edit migration SQL files. Establish a team workflow where developers pull and apply migrations before creating new ones.

faq.question: Why am I getting too many connections errors in production?
faq.answer: Your connection pool is exhausted. Add connection_limit to your DATABASE_URL (e.g., connection_limit=5). For serverless, use a global PrismaClient instance to reuse connections. For high-traffic apps, consider PgBouncer or your cloud providers connection pooling.

faq.question: Can Prisma work with existing databases?
faq.answer: Yes. Use npx prisma db pull to introspect an existing database and generate a schema. Review and customize the generated schema, then run npx prisma generate to create the client. You can also use prisma migrate to manage future schema changes.

faq.question: How do I handle database transactions in Prisma?
faq.answer: Use prisma.$transaction with an array for sequential operations, or a callback function for interactive transactions with business logic. Configure maxWait, timeout, and isolationLevel options. Be aware that interactive transactions hold connections longer.
